subspaces.name: Quantum Chromodynamics
subspaces.description: The quantum field theory describing the strong interaction between quarks and gluons, governing hadron structure, confinement, and asymptotic freedom.
subspaces.probe_query: quantum chromodynamics QCD confinement asymptotic freedom parton distribution functions lattice QCD

subspaces.name: Electroweak Theory
subspaces.description: The unified quantum field theory of electromagnetic and weak interactions, incorporating spontaneous symmetry breaking via the Higgs mechanism.
subspaces.probe_query: electroweak theory Weinberg-Salam model Higgs mechanism W/Z bosons neutral current electroweak precision tests

subspaces.name: Beyond Standard Model Physics
subspaces.description: Theoretical and experimental frameworks extending or replacing the Standard Model to address its known limitations—neutrino mass, dark matter, baryogenesis, hierarchy problem, and gravity unification.
subspaces.probe_query: beyond Standard Model BSM supersymmetry grand unification axions leptoquarks

subspaces.name: Collider Phenomenology
subspaces.description: Interpretation of collider data (especially LHC, Tevatron, future colliders) through theoretical models, simulation, statistical inference, and signature-based searches for new particles or interactions.
seed_papers: de Florian et al. (2016) Rep. Prog. Phys. 79:096201
subspaces.probe_query: collider phenomenology LHC physics event generation Monte Carlo simulation trigger strategies signature-based search

subspaces.name: Neutrino Physics
subspaces.description: Study of neutrino properties—including mass, mixing, CP violation, and Majorana vs. Dirac nature—via oscillation experiments, double-beta decay, and astrophysical sources.
seed_papers: Capozzi et al. (2016) Phys. Rev. D 95:096014
subspaces.probe_query: neutrino oscillations PMNS matrix neutrino mass hierarchy neutrinoless double beta decay atmospheric/solar/reactor neutrinos DUNE

subspaces.name: Heavy Flavor Physics
subspaces.description: Precision study of quark flavor transitions involving bottom and charm quarks to test Standard Model predictions, constrain CKM parameters, and probe BSM effects.
subspaces.probe_query: heavy flavor physics B physics CKM matrix CP violation rare decays B factories

subspaces.name: Astroparticle Physics
subspaces.description: Interface of particle physics with astrophysics and cosmology—studying high-energy cosmic rays, gamma rays, neutrinos, and gravitational waves to probe fundamental interactions and dark matter.
seed_papers: Gaisser et al. (2016) Cosmic Rays and Particle Physics
Aharonian et al. (2022) CTA Consortium White Paper arXiv:2207.05122
subspaces.probe_query: astroparticle physics cosmic rays gamma-ray astronomy high-energy neutrinos Pierre Auger Observatory Fermi-LAT

subspaces.name: Lattice Quantum Field Theory
subspaces.description: Non-perturbative computational approach using discretized spacetime lattices to solve QCD and other quantum field theories from first principles.
seed_papers: Bazavov et al. (2019) Phys. Rev. D 100:054517
subspaces.probe_query: lattice QCD lattice field theory Wilson fermions chiral symmetry finite temperature QCD hadron masses

subspaces.name: Quantum Gravity and String Phenomenology
subspaces.description: Efforts to connect quantum gravity frameworks—especially string theory and AdS/CFT—with observable low-energy phenomena, including inflation, black hole signatures, and modified dispersion relations.
seed_papers: Baumann & McAllister (2015) Inflation and String Theory
subspaces.probe_query: string phenomenology quantum gravity phenomenology AdS/CFT correspondence swampland program primordial gravitational waves black hole information paradox

subspaces.name: High-Energy Nuclear Physics
subspaces.description: Study of strongly interacting matter under extreme temperature and density—primarily via relativistic heavy-ion collisions—to probe deconfinement, chiral symmetry restoration, and collective QCD dynamics.
seed_papers: Busza et al. (2018) Ann. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 68:339
Gale et al. (2013) Ann. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 63:73
subspaces.probe_query: quark-gluon plasma QGP relativistic heavy-ion collisions hydrodynamic flow jet quenching RHIC

user_interaction.question: Select the subspaces to prioritize before ZhiZhi imports papers. You can also add custom subspaces.

options.label: Quantum Chromodynamics
options.description: The quantum field theory describing the strong interaction between quarks and gluons, governing hadron structure, confinement, and asymptotic freedom.

options.label: Electroweak Theory
options.description: The unified quantum field theory of electromagnetic and weak interactions, incorporating spontaneous symmetry breaking via the Higgs mechanism.

options.label: Beyond Standard Model Physics
options.description: Theoretical and experimental frameworks extending or replacing the Standard Model to address its known limitations—neutrino mass, dark matter, baryogenesis, hierarchy problem, and gravity unification.

options.label: Collider Phenomenology
options.description: Interpretation of collider data (especially LHC, Tevatron, future colliders) through theoretical models, simulation, statistical inference, and signature-based searches for new particles or interactions.

options.label: Neutrino Physics
options.description: Study of neutrino properties—including mass, mixing, CP violation, and Majorana vs. Dirac nature—via oscillation experiments, double-beta decay, and astrophysical sources.

options.label: Heavy Flavor Physics
options.description: Precision study of quark flavor transitions involving bottom and charm quarks to test Standard Model predictions, constrain CKM parameters, and probe BSM effects.

options.label: Astroparticle Physics
options.description: Interface of particle physics with astrophysics and cosmology—studying high-energy cosmic rays, gamma rays, neutrinos, and gravitational waves to probe fundamental interactions and dark matter.

options.label: Lattice Quantum Field Theory
options.description: Non-perturbative computational approach using discretized spacetime lattices to solve QCD and other quantum field theories from first principles.

options.label: Quantum Gravity and String Phenomenology
options.description: Efforts to connect quantum gravity frameworks—especially string theory and AdS/CFT—with observable low-energy phenomena, including inflation, black hole signatures, and modified dispersion relations.

options.label: High-Energy Nuclear Physics
options.description: Study of strongly interacting matter under extreme temperature and density—primarily via relativistic heavy-ion collisions—to probe deconfinement, chiral symmetry restoration, and collective QCD dynamics.

custom_subspace_input.placeholder: e.g. Demand Response; EV Charging Coordination; Building Energy Management
custom_subspace_input.instructions: If your target subfield is not listed, provide one subspace per line or semicolon-separated. These will be converted into custom retrieval branches.

user_interaction.continue_with: Pass subspace_map_id plus selected_subfields, or pass option labels as focus_branches to run_zhizhi_literature_analysis.
